mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary pressures that systematically reduce corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal income and housing starts indicates weakening consumer demand and construction activity, which jointly suppresses corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation above 205, the Federal Reserve pauses rate hikes to avoid economic damage, triggering a relief rally that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 205 during tightening cycles, or the relief rally pattern disappears in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate capacity utilization, which directly supports earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in <45% of periods when INDPRO rises, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150 thousand signal robust labor demand and consumer spending power, which systematically boosts corporate profit forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of months where PAYEMS growth surpasses 150 thousand, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant real GDP growth combined with accelerating inflation creates a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of flat GDPC1 and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 60% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment remains below 4.5 percent, tight labor markets force wage increases that initially boost consumer spending, generating short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week before inflation concerns dominate.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE stays below 4.5 during early-cycle phases, or the short-term positive signal reverses in walk-forward testing.
